subject: ap-calculus-ab
title: Escalator Line — Entry Rate, People in Line & Minimum (Table/Rate)
stimulus:
People enter a line for an escalator at a rate modeled by the function $r$ given by
$$r(t) = \begin{cases} 44\left(\frac{t}{100}\right)^3\left(1 - \frac{t}{300}\right)^7 & \text{for } 0 \le t \le 300 \\ 0 & \text{for } t > 300 \end{cases}$$
where $r(t)$ is measured in people per second and $t$ is measured in seconds. As people get on the escalator, they exit the line at a constant rate of $0.7$ person per second. There are $20$ people in line at time $t = 0$.

parts.prompt: How many people enter the line for the escalator during the time interval $0 \le t \le 300$?
parts.scoring_notes: From scoring key: $\int_0^{300} r(t)\, dt = 270$. According to the model, 270 people enter the line during $0 \le t \le 300$.

parts.prompt: During the time interval $0 \le t \le 300$, there are always people in line for the escalator. How many people are in line at time $t = 300$?

scoring_points.description: Considers rate out: accounts for $0.7$ person per second exiting; $20 + \int_0^{300} r(t)\, dt - 0.7 \cdot 300$

required_elements: Considers rate out: accounts for $0.7$ person per second exiting; $20 + \int_0^{300} r(t)\, dt - 0.7 \cdot 300$

parts.prompt: For $t > 300$, what is the first time $t$ that there are no people in line for the escalator?
parts.scoring_notes: From scoring key: After $t = 300$, $r(t) = 0$ and people leave at $0.7$ per second. With $80$ people at $t = 300$, the line empties at $t = 300 + \frac{80}{0.7} \approx 414.286$ seconds.

parts.prompt: For $0 \le t \le 300$, at what time $t$ is the number of people in line a minimum? To the nearest whole number, find the number of people in line at this time. Justify your answer.
parts.scoring_notes: From scoring key: $r(t) - 0.7 = 0$ gives $t_1 \approx 33.013$ and $t_2 \approx 166.575$. Values: $t=0 \to 20$, $t_1 \to \approx 3.803$, $t_2 \to \approx 158.070$, $t=300 \to 80$. Minimum at $t \approx 33.013$ seconds with approximately $4$ people (rounded to nearest whole number).

scoring_points.description: Identifies $t \approx 33.013$ as the critical point (and $t_2 \approx 166.575$); the number of people in line at $t_1$ is a minimum

required_elements: Identifies $t \approx 33.013$ as the critical point (and $t_2 \approx 166.575$); the number of people in line at $t_1$ is a minimum

scoring_points.description: Correct answer: minimum of approximately $4$ people at $t \approx 33.013$ seconds

required_elements: Correct answer: minimum of approximately $4$ people at $t \approx 33.013$ seconds

scoring_points.description: Justification: tests critical points and endpoints; values are $20$ (at $t=0$), $\approx 3.803$ (at $t_1$), $\approx 158.070$ (at $t_2$), $80$ (at $t=300$); minimum is at $t \approx 33.013$

required_elements: endpoints; values are $20$ (at $t=0$), $\approx 3.803$ (at $t_1$), $\approx 158.070$ (at $t_2$), $80$ (at $t=300$); minimum is at $t \approx 33.013$
